name: Colorado River Below Glenwood Springs, CO
notes: Lower Glenwood Colorado mainstem. Wider boat-friendly prime window than smaller tributaries.

name: Colorado R Abv Roaring Fork R at Glenwood Spgs, CO
notes: Above the Roaring Fork confluence. Slightly smaller and more manageable than below-Glenwood Colorado.

name: Colorado River Above Glenwood Springs, CO
notes: Upper Glenwood Colorado reference. Similar to the above-Roaring-Fork station.

name: Colorado R Blw South Canyon CR NR Glenwood Spgs CO
notes: South Canyon Colorado reach. Still good with push, but less forgiving for casual wading once above prime.

name: Grizzly Creek NR Grizzly Lake NR Glenwood Spgs, CO
notes: Small high-elevation Grizzly Creek tributary. Best used as a runoff contribution signal rather than a direct fishing recommendation.

name: Roaring Fork River at Glenwood Springs, Co.
notes: Lower Roaring Fork around Glenwood. Prime range widened a bit from the old defaults based on guide reports.

name: CO River near dotsero
notes: Dotsero Colorado reference. Narrower and easier to push out than the bigger Colorado below Glenwood.

name: Fryingpan River Near Ruedi, Co.
notes: Ruedi tailwater. Excellent tailwater window is much lower than freestones and big mainstem rivers.

name: Roaring Fork River Near Aspen, CO
notes: Upper Roaring Fork above Aspen. Smaller, colder, and easier to push out than the Emma or Glenwood reaches.

name: Roaring Fork River Blw Maroon Creek NR Aspen, CO
notes: Roaring Fork below Maroon Creek near Aspen. Bigger than the upper Aspen gauge, but still meaningfully smaller and more technical than Emma.

name: Roaring Fork River Near Emma, CO
notes: Emma reach Roaring Fork. Classic wade-friendly middle Fork range.

name: Crystal River Abv Avalanche Crk, Near Redstone, CO
notes: Upper Crystal freestone. Mid-summer reports suggest the river fishes very well in the 40-80 cfs neighborhood.

name: Colorado River Near Cameo, Co.
notes: Big lower Colorado near Cameo. Needs more volume than Glenwood-area stations before it feels ideal.

name: Elk Creek at New Castle, Co.
notes: Starter thresholds for a smaller tributary gauge. Lower confidence and worth revisiting with local observations.

name: Parachute Creek at Parachute, Co.
notes: Starter thresholds for a smaller western tributary gauge. Lower confidence and worth revisiting with local observations.

name: Roan Creek at DE Beque, Co.
notes: Starter thresholds for Roan Creek at De Beque. Small lower-valley tributary with meaningful temperature and turbidity context.
